doc_description: This document presents a comprehensive analysis of a clinical dataset on gastrointestinal diseases, detailing demographic, clinical, and symptom-related variables from 30,560 patients, along with a data dictionary, global statistical overview, disease class distribution, and detailed patient record samples.

structure.title: Clinical Dataset Analysis: Gastrointestinal Diseases
structure.summary: The partial document provides an overview of a clinical dataset analysis focused on gastrointestinal diseases, encompassing data from 30,560 patients. It details 37 variables related to demographics, symptoms, lab values (such as CRP and Fecal Calprotectin), and diagnostic outcomes. Key sections include a data dictionary that outlines features categorized into demographics (age, gender, ethnicity, BMI), clinical markers (inflammation indicators), symptoms (binary flags for abdominal pain, bloating, and diarrhea), and diagnosis (final labeled gastrointestinal conditions).
structure.text:
Clinical Dataset Analysis: Gastrointestinal Diseases
This report contains structured clinical data for 30560 patients. It covers 37 variables including demographics, symptoms, lab values (CRP, Fecal Calprotectin), and
diagnostic outcomes.
1. Data Dictionary & Feature Engineering
Category
Features
Description
Demographics
Age, Gender, Ethnicity, BMI
Basic patient profile and physical markers.
Clinical Markers
CRP_ESR, Fecal Calprotectin
Inflammation and biochemical indicators.
Symptoms
Abdominal Pain, Bloating, Diarrhea
Binary flags (0/1) for reported symptoms.
Diagnosis
Disease_Class
The final labeled gastrointestinal condition.

nodes.title: Data Dictionary & Feature Engineering
nodes.summary: The partial document provides an analysis of a clinical dataset focused on gastrointestinal diseases, encompassing data from 30,560 patients and detailing 37 variables related to demographics, symptoms, lab values, and diagnostic outcomes. Key sections include a data dictionary that outlines features such as demographics (age, gender, ethnicity, BMI), clinical markers (CRP, fecal calprotectin), symptoms (abdominal pain, bloating, diarrhea), and diagnosis (disease classification). Additionally, a global statistical overview presents descriptive statistics for various features, including counts, means, standard deviations, and percentiles for variables like age, BMI, inflammation markers, and reported symptoms. This comprehensive analysis aims to facilitate understanding and research into gastrointestinal conditions.

structure.title: Global Statistical Overview
structure.summary: The partial document provides a global statistical overview of various health-related features based on a sample size of 30,560 individuals. It includes descriptive statistics such as count, mean, standard deviation, minimum, and maximum values for several health metrics, including age, BMI, body weight, family history, genetic markers, microbiome index, autoimmune disorders, H. Pylori status, fecal calprotectin, and various health symptoms like abdominal pain, bloating, diarrhea, and constipation. Additionally, it presents data on lifestyle factors such as smoking status, alcohol use, stress level, and physical activity. The document also includes a disease class distribution section, detailing the patient count for specific symptoms and conditions, such as abdominal cramps, blood in stool, nausea, unexplained weight loss, and diarrhea or constipation.

structure.title: Disease Class Distribution
structure.summary: The partial document covers the usage statistics of various medications, including NSAIDs, antibiotics, and PPIs, along with their respective usage rates. It also presents a distribution of disease types among patients, highlighting common symptoms such as abdominal cramps, blood in stool, nausea, and unexplained weight loss, with patient counts for each symptom. Additionally, the document includes detailed patient record samples, organized by demographic and health-related features such as age, gender, BMI, body weight, obesity status, ethnicity, family history, genetic markers, and microbiome index.

structure.title: Patient Record Samples (Detailed View)
structure.summary: The partial document provides a detailed view of patient records organized into logical feature groups for improved readability. It covers various health-related attributes, including demographic information (age, gender, BMI, body weight, obesity status, ethnicity, family history, genetic markers, and microbiome index), autoimmune disorders, H. Pylori status, fecal calprotectin levels, dietary habits, and lifestyle factors such as food intolerance, smoking status, alcohol use, stress level, and physical activity. Additionally, it includes gastrointestinal symptoms like abdominal pain, bloating, diarrhea, constipation, rectal bleeding, appetite loss, and weight loss, along with bowel habits and medication usage. The document also lists disease classes associated with the symptoms reported by patients, highlighting common issues such as unexplained weight loss, nausea, and blood in stool.
